title: T-027 — Field primitive — RHF Controller + Zod resolver wrapper with label/hint/error/required states
prompt:
# T-027 — Field primitive — RHF Controller + Zod resolver wrapper with label/hint/error/required states

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5.y, §5
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Field primitive wrapping React Hook Form Controller + Zod resolver. Renders label, hint, error message, required-mark, inline-validation states.

## Implementation contract
packages/ui/src/Field.tsx: props = { name, label, hint?, required?, type, schema? }. Internally uses useFormContext() (caller wraps with FormProvider) and Controller. Exposes 'error', 'success', 'idle' states via aria-invalid + aria-describedby. Inline validation triggers on blur and on form submit. Required-mark is a visible asterisk wrapped in <span aria-label='required'>. The Settings invite-modal in access-screens.jsx:139-145 (Email/Role/Site fields) is the canonical structural reference. Field.test.tsx parity asserts the same label-on-top + input-below + hint-below structure as access-screens.jsx:139-145.

## Prototype parity
Canonical anchor: `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/access-screens.jsx:139-145`.
Rationale: spec-driven primitive (PRD §5.y `Field` = RHF Controller + Zod resolver wrapper); nearest reusable prototype excerpt is the input/label/hint row pattern inside `users_screen`.
Closeout must demonstrate structural, visual, and interaction parity per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md` (RTL snapshot + parity checklist + screenshots; axe a11y check).

## Files (scope)
- packages/ui/src/Field.tsx [create]
- packages/ui/.storybook/Field.stories.tsx [create]
- packages/ui/src/__tests__/Field.test.tsx [create]
- prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/access-screens.jsx:139-145 [ref]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the Field primitive renders, when compared to the invite-modal field block in prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/access-screens.jsx:139-145, then it matches structurally (label on top, input below, optional hint/error below), visually (uses shadcn Input as the inner primitive — no raw <input>), and interactionally (validation runs on blur and on submit, error message replaces hint when present, aria-invalid='true' is set on error) — verified by Field.test.tsx
2. Given a Zod schema rejects the value, when the user blurs the field, then aria-invalid='true' is set and the resolver error message renders below the input
3. Given required=true, when the field renders, then a visible asterisk is present with aria-label='required'

## Test strategy
- RED: write packages/ui/src/__tests__/Field.test.tsx asserting parity + a11y before Field.tsx exists
- Run 'pnpm --filter @monopilot/ui test'

## Risk red lines
- Do not render a raw <input> — must use the shadcn Input wrapper to keep visual parity with prototypes

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-025

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.
